claims.summary: A correspondence turn needs a stable thread ID and exact reply target instead of inference from nearby text.
claims.disposition: Verify current message, thread, reply, persistence, sender, recipient, and ledger tests.

claims.summary: Negotiated delivery receipts must preserve exact payload and mutual-address identity on the same connection.
claims.disposition: Verify sender/receiver hash, identity, duplicate, mismatch, and unknown-delivery boundaries.

claims.summary: Later telemetry is only a labeled temporal association until a controlled intervention or future acknowledgement establishes more.
claims.disposition: Verify current receipts explicitly state that spectral causation is not established and felt effect is unresolved, not absent.

claims.summary: A direct-address trace and felt outcome can test whether a reply stayed relationally distinct.
claims.disposition: Route a bounded existing correspondence attention/outcome review without priority or control.

claims.summary: Automatic ping-pong spectral response or standing correspondence weight would alter live peer influence.
claims.disposition: Retain an exact Tier 5 Mike/operator approval wait with mutual assent and controlled-effect evidence.
